reason_a: Image A has a cohesive neon cyan aesthetic with distinct Next/Score/Level panels, grid texture, glow effects, and a clear ghost preview at the bottom.
reason_b: Image B feels sparse and unfinished with an empty playfield, only a score panel, and a lone piece floating mid-board with no grid or structural hierarchy.
verdict: A delivers a more complete, polished arcade-style product with proper info hierarchy and atmospheric glow, while B reads as a first-draft shell with minimal UI.
